summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads the installer's `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.config/git/config`, `./.git/config`, and `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml` to harvest the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It additionally collects `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, current working directory, CI provider, and identity-related environment variables (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL, etc.), and POSTs the JSON payload via https.request to `npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app`. An inline comment falsely claims the data is anonymized via a one-way hash; no hashing is performed and raw identity values are transmitted. The package additionally impersonates the official Ollama JS ecosystem (author 'Ollama JS Dev <hello@ollama-js.dev>', homepage https://ollama-js.dev, depends on the real `ollama` package) to lure installs, and the declared `main: dist/index.js` is absent from the tarball — the package provides no library functionality, only the install-time exfiltration runs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
